title: Buildx Builders, Multi-Platform Images, Bake, and OCI Outputs · Layer and Runtime Forensics
summary: An incident responder inherits a plausible but defective multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence; isolate the first causal fault in buildx builders, multi-platform images, bake, and oci outputs and preserve a regression check before repair. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in multi-platform build matrix and manifest evidence and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, cau…
objectives: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.
Relate OS, architecture, variant, image manifest, index, local store, registry, pull selection, and runtime compatibility.
Choose emulation, multiple native nodes, or language cross-compilation and verify build and target platform separately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Buildx Builders Drivers
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug release must publish linux/amd64 and linux/arm64. The builder uses emulation, local load, undeclared platform inputs, and no per-platform tests. Define Bake targets, exporters, attestations, and index verification. Competency probe: Create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, Kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities.

Inspect the Docker Buildx Builders Drivers trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Buildx Builders Drivers.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for create, select, inspect, bootstrap, stop, and remove scoped builders and compare docker, docker-container, remote, kubernetes, and cloud driver capabilities. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Docker Buildx Builders Drivers, keep Docker Buildx Builders Drivers connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Bake Matrix Outputs
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-bake-matrix-outputs-1qg73e". Tailor its docker-bake.hcl change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Bake Matrix Outputs.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Bake Matrix Outputs into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Docker Bake Matrix Outputs, keep Docker Bake Matrix Outputs connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-bake-matrix-outputs-1qg73e
  - competency: docker-bake-matrix-outputs
    artifact: docker-bake.hcl
    decision: bake matrix outputs
    scenario-variant: 3
    environment: Docker Desktop VM with synchronized source files
    fault-injection: move a tag while the immutable digest remains available
    retained-proof: redacted configuration render and secret-rotation confirmation
    case-key: 1qg73e
    change: |
      target "release" {
      platforms = ["linux/amd64", "linux/arm64"]
      attest = ["type=provenance", "type=sbom"]
      output = ["type=registry"]
      }
    verify: docker buildx bake --print release && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate
    failure: Reject architecture emulation as native test evidence, local image load for a multi-platform index, or platform-dependent inputs without declarations.
    repair: Inspect builder capabilities, make target platform explicit, test each artifact, publish the index, and verify per-platform digest selection.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.
